regression_risks: Nondeterministic admission: _report_id_source_id (adapter_error_frontier.py:685-689) selects the source id by first frozenset-iterated event marker that is a substring, via leftmost partition, not by anchored/leftmost-position parse. Any report_id containing two distinct event-kind markers yields per-process-variable extraction (PYTHONHASHSEED str-hash randomization). For report-thread rows whose source id embeds an event-kind token this can collapse to '<expected>' or 'brick-protocol-<expected>' and FALSE-ADMIT a foreign building's root as declaration_chain_only -> effective_overwrite path (line ~250) could overwrite a different/partial root.

This is the same collision class the contract requires closed ('reject both prefix and suffix/infix collisions'): the prefix and trailing-suffix directions are closed, but the embedded-event-marker infix direction is not.
Checker stability risk: a single added multi-marker probe could pass or fail depending on PYTHONHASHSEED, i.e. flaky GREEN/RED for the same code.
